summary: Response time for these taxpayer-advocate inquiries has swung repeatedly for different documented reasons each year: a 2023 change in how DOF defines an "inquiry" (no longer auto-escalated to a case after 10 days) initially drove times up, a 2023 state law changing income rules for homeowner exemptions added verification work in fiscal 2024, DOF's process improvements brought times down sharply in fiscal 2025, and the office's growing involvement in complex lien-sale-related inquiries pushed times back up again afterward.

sources.label: NYC Department of Finance, Fiscal 2023 Mayor's Management Report
NYC Department of Finance, Fiscal 2024 Mayor's Management Report
NYC Department of Finance, Fiscal 2025 Mayor's Management Report
NYC Department of Finance, Fiscal 2026 Preliminary Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing, though this has swung for several distinct documented reasons each year
